NousResearch/hermes-agent.summary: Hermes Agent is an autonomous AI system designed for persistent, cross-platform task execution and self-improvement. It utilizes a closed learning loop to autonomously generate new skills from experience and build long-term user models through periodic memory nudges and session searches. The agent operates across 14 messaging platforms and supports six execution backends, including Docker and SSH, for secure tool use. Users can switch between various LLM providers and automate recurring tasks via a built-in natural language cron scheduler.
highlights: Autonomous skill creation and self-improvement from task experience
Supports 14 messaging platforms including Telegram, Discord, and Slack
Six execution backends for local, containerized, or serverless persistence

NousResearch/Hermes-Function-Calling.summary: This repository provides the official implementation and examples for function calling and JSON mode using the Hermes 2 Pro model. It utilizes a recursive loop to generate, execute, and process tool calls based on structured schemas, specifically supporting financial data retrieval via yfinance. Users can define custom tools using Python decorators or enforce structured outputs by serializing Pydantic models into JSON schemas. The project demonstrates multi-turn interactions using the ChatML format to maintain state between tool responses and assistant replies.
highlights: Enables recursive function calling and structured JSON mode inference
Supports custom tool definition via Python decorators and Pydantic
Uses ChatML prompt format for OpenAI-compatible multi-turn dialogue

NousResearch/atropos.summary: Atropos is an environment microservice framework designed for asynchronous reinforcement learning with large language models. It operates by setting up environments as services that communicate via a trajectory API, allowing trainers to pull data batches while models interact with external tools. The system supports diverse training scenarios including code execution, multi-turn tool calling, and multimodal tasks. It has been used to significantly improve model performance on benchmarks like the Berkeley Function Calling Benchmark.
highlights: Microservice architecture for scalable asynchronous reinforcement learning environments
Standardized trajectory API for collecting and distributing model interaction data
Supports code execution, game-based learning, and RLAIF reward models

NousResearch/hermes-agent-self-evolution.summary: Hermes Agent Self-Evolution is an automated optimization framework designed to improve the performance of Hermes Agent without requiring GPU training. The system utilizes DSPy and Genetic-Pareto Prompt Evolution (GEPA) to analyze execution traces and mutate skill files, prompts, and tool descriptions. It validates candidate improvements through a rigorous pipeline of synthetic or session-based evaluations, constraint gates, and mandatory test suites. The final output is a refined version of the agent's components delivered via pull requests for human review.
highlights: Optimizes skills and prompts using reflective evolutionary search via API calls.
Analyzes execution traces to understand and fix specific failure points.
Enforces strict guardrails including size limits and 100% test pass rates.

NousResearch/hermes-paperclip-adapter.summary: The hermes-paperclip-adapter is a bridge that integrates Hermes Agent into the Paperclip company management system as a managed employee. It functions by wrapping the Hermes CLI, parsing raw terminal output into structured transcript entries and GFM markdown for clean rendering in the Paperclip UI. The adapter enables agents to respond to task assignments or issue comments while maintaining persistent memory and session continuity across heartbeats. It also synchronizes local and managed skills, allowing Hermes to utilize its full suite of 30+ native tools and MCP servers within a corporate organizational structure.
highlights: Connects Hermes Agent to Paperclip as a managed autonomous employee
Parses raw CLI output into structured tool cards and markdown
Supports 8 inference providers including Anthropic, OpenAI, and OpenRouter

NousResearch/autonovel.summary: Autonovel is an autonomous agent pipeline designed to transform a seed concept into a complete, publishable novel. It utilizes a multi-phase workflow that includes world-building, sequential drafting, and an adversarial revision loop involving dual-persona LLM reviewers. The system integrates mechanical slop detection and voice fingerprinting to ensure prose quality and narrative consistency across tens of thousands of words. Beyond text generation, the project automates typesetting in LaTeX, cover art creation via fal.ai, and multi-voice audiobook production using ElevenLabs.
highlights: Autonomous four-phase pipeline from world-building to final export
Adversarial revision loop with mechanical and LLM-based evaluation
Automated typesetting, cover illustration, and multi-voice audiobook generation

outsourc-e/hermes-workspace.summary: Hermes Workspace is a native web-based command center designed to provide a unified graphical interface for the Hermes Agent ecosystem. It integrates directly with the Hermes gateway via real-time SSE streaming to manage chat, terminal access, and tool call rendering. Users can browse and edit agent memory, manage over 2,000 skills, and orchestrate complex jobs through a security-hardened dashboard. The project follows a zero-fork architecture, allowing it to run on vanilla installations of the Nous Research hermes-agent without custom patches.
highlights: Real-time SSE streaming for chat and tool call rendering
Comprehensive memory browser and skill manager for agent orchestration
Security-hardened interface with execution approval prompts and auth middleware

fathah/hermes-desktop.summary: Hermes Desktop is a native GUI companion designed to simplify the installation, configuration, and management of the Hermes Agent AI assistant. It replaces manual CLI management by providing a guided setup process and a centralized interface for interacting with the agent. Users can manage multiple LLM providers, configure messaging gateways, and control complex toolsets through a visual workspace. The application also includes features for session history, memory management, and scheduled task automation.
highlights: Guided installation and dependency resolution for Hermes Agent
Support for diverse LLM providers and local endpoints
Integrated messaging gateways for platforms like Discord and Telegram

sanchomuzax/hermes-webui.summary: Hermes WebUI is a process monitoring and configuration dashboard designed to provide a visual interface for the Hermes Agent. It functions as a non-intrusive polling bridge that reads from the agent's SQLite database and YAML files to display real-time activity without requiring modifications to the core agent code. Users can monitor session histories, track costs, inspect skill source code, and manage scheduled cron jobs through a responsive, themeable web interface. The system ensures security by providing read-only access to environment variables and requiring token-based authentication for access.
highlights: Real-time activity monitoring with session tracking and cost distribution
Full conversation history inspection with FTS5 search capabilities
Read-only configuration viewing and scheduled cron job management

Euraika-Labs/pan-ui.summary: Pan is a self-hosted web dashboard and workspace designed to provide a graphical interface for the Hermes Agent runtime. It functions as a Next.js server that bridges the browser to the Hermes API, automatically managing the agent gateway and providing a centralized control plane. Users can manage isolated profiles, browse and install skills from skills.sh, and monitor real-time tool executions through a streaming chat interface. The platform also includes dedicated management views for Model Context Protocol (MCP) integrations, memory inspection, and system-wide audit trails.
highlights: Chat-first workspace with SSE streaming and tool execution timelines
Integrated marketplace for skills.sh, MCP servers, and plugins
Isolated profile management for separate sessions, memory, and keys

mukul975/Anthropic-Cybersecurity-Skills.summary: Anthropic Cybersecurity Skills is a library of 754 structured security skills designed to provide AI agents with the domain-specific workflows of a senior analyst. It utilizes the agentskills.io open standard, providing YAML-frontmatter for discovery and structured Markdown for step-by-step execution across 26 security domains. Each skill is uniquely mapped to five industry frameworks, including MITRE ATT&CK, NIST CSF 2.0, and the NIST AI RMF. The project is compatible with major AI platforms such as Claude Code, GitHub Copilot, and Hermes Agent to assist in threat hunting and incident response.
highlights: 754 structured skills mapped to MITRE ATT&CK and NIST CSF 2.0
Covers 26 security domains including Cloud, Malware Analysis, and ICS
Compatible with agentskills.io standard for seamless AI agent integration

conorbronsdon/avoid-ai-writing.summary: avoid-ai-writing is a portable skill for AI agents designed to identify and eliminate detectable AI writing patterns. It utilizes a structured audit process featuring 36 pattern categories and a three-tier word replacement table to replace AI-isms with plainer language. The tool operates in either a rewrite mode with a built-in second-pass audit or a detection-only mode for content scanning. It is compatible with agentskills.io-compliant agents, including Claude Code, OpenClaw, and Hermes.
highlights: Two-pass audit to catch surviving AI patterns after rewriting
109-entry word replacement table across three severity tiers
Compatible with Claude Code, OpenClaw, and Hermes Agent

wondelai/skills.summary: Wondel.ai Skills is a cross-platform library of agent capabilities designed for Claude Code and agentskills.io-compatible environments. It provides pre-configured skill collections based on established industry frameworks, such as Jobs to Be Done, Clean Code, and Lean Startup methodology. Users can install these skills via the Claude Code marketplace or a dedicated CLI to equip AI agents with specialized knowledge in software architecture, UX design, and product strategy. The project bridges the gap between raw LLM capabilities and structured professional methodologies through modular, installable plugins.
highlights: Compatible with Claude Code and agentskills.io-compatible agents
Modular installation via marketplace or npx skills CLI
Frameworks for architecture, UX design, marketing, and product strategy

DougTrajano/pydantic-ai-skills.summary: pydantic-ai-skills is a framework that implements the Agent Skills open specification to provide modular, specialized capabilities for Pydantic AI agents. It uses a tool-calling approach to enable progressive disclosure, allowing agents to discover and load detailed instructions or scripts only when relevant to a task. The package supports skills defined as filesystem directories or created programmatically using Python decorators and dataclasses. This architecture reduces token consumption by preventing the injection of unnecessary context into the initial system prompt.
highlights: Implements the Agent Skills open specification for Pydantic AI
Reduces token usage through progressive disclosure of skill metadata
Supports both filesystem-based and programmatic Python skill definitions

Agents365-ai/drawio-skill.summary: The drawio-skill project is an agentic tool that converts natural language descriptions into professional .drawio diagrams. It utilizes a set of six diagram presets and a grid-aligned layout engine to generate XML files that can be exported to PNG, SVG, or PDF via the draw.io desktop CLI. The system features a self-correcting iterative loop that reads exported files to auto-fix layout issues and supports specialized ML model diagrams with tensor shape annotations. It integrates across multiple AI platforms including Claude Code, OpenClaw, and Hermes Agent.
highlights: Generates six diagram types including UML, ERD, and ML architectures
Supports animated connectors and 10px grid-aligned coordinate snapping
Automates iterative refinement with a 5-round self-check safety valve

smartcontractkit/chainlink-agent-skills.summary: Chainlink Agent Skills is a collection of standardized tools that enable AI agents to interact with Chainlink oracle services and smart contracts. The project implements the Agent Skills specification, allowing agents to discover and execute specific blockchain functions through a modular CLI-based installation. These skills provide capabilities for cross-chain transfers via CCIP, data feed integration, and Chainlink Runtime Environment (CRE) operations. By using these skills, developers can prompt agents to generate Solidity code, manage workflows, and perform local testing for decentralized applications.
highlights: Implements standardized Agent Skills specification for AI agent interoperability.
Enables CCIP cross-chain sends, monitoring, and local testing workflows.
Provides multi-chain Data Feeds integration and CRE runtime operations.

tlehman/litprog-skill.summary: The litprog-skill is a Claude Code extension that implements Donald Knuth's literate programming paradigm by transforming codebases into human-readable narratives. It works by generating a .lit.md file that weaves together prose, Mermaid diagrams, and LaTeX math with the original source code. The tool includes a tangler to extract runnable files and a reverse-sync engine to ensure the documentation stays updated when source files are edited. This approach helps developers document architectural decisions and facilitates easier onboarding through structured, essay-like code explanations.
highlights: Generates .lit.md files combining prose, diagrams, and source code
Extracts runnable source files from documentation using a tangler
Automatically syncs source file edits back to the literate document

esaradev/icarus-plugin.summary: Icarus is a Hermes plugin designed to create self-improving agents through shared memory and automated model fine-tuning. It captures agent decisions, session outcomes, and structured notes into a markdown-based "fabric" directory that can be optionally managed via Obsidian. This data is then used to extract high-quality training pairs for fine-tuning cheaper replacement models via Together AI. The system includes a full pipeline for evaluating candidate models against base versions and switching to them once performance thresholds are met.
highlights: Cross-instance shared memory via markdown-based fabric directories
Automated training data extraction with decision-quality tagging
End-to-end pipeline for fine-tuning and evaluating replacement models

chigwell/skilldock.io.summary: SkillDock is a registry and SDK for managing reusable AI skills based on the AgentSkills specification. It provides a Python client and CLI that interact with an OpenAPI-driven backend to discover, install, and version agent capabilities. The platform supports a commerce layer for buying and selling skills using Stripe or TON blockchain payments. Developers can package local skill folders, manage dependencies, and publish immutable releases with metadata defined in Markdown frontmatter.
highlights: OpenAPI-driven Python SDK and CLI for skill lifecycle management
Supports skill monetization via Stripe and TON blockchain integration
Automated dependency resolution and local installation of AI skills

black-forest-labs/skills.summary: This project provides official image generation skills for FLUX models, offering standardized prompting guidelines and API integration patterns following the agentskills.io specification. It functions as a technical resource for developers to implement text-to-image and image-to-image workflows through structured JSON prompting, hex color specification, and multi-reference editing. The repository includes a model selection guide for FLUX.1 and FLUX.2 variants and can be integrated directly into Claude Code as a plugin. Users can access documented API patterns for asynchronous polling, webhook verification, and rate limit management.
highlights: Standardized prompting guidelines for FLUX.1 and FLUX.2 model families
API integration patterns for polling, webhooks, and error handling
Support for JSON structured prompting and precise hex color specification

Romanescu11/hermes-skill-factory.summary: Skill Factory is a meta-skill for Hermes Agent that automates the creation of reusable skills by monitoring user workflows. It functions by tracking session patterns in the background and proposing structured skill definitions when it detects repeatable sequences. Users can generate both SKILL.md documentation and Python plugin scaffolds directly from these proposals via slash commands or natural language. This tool streamlines the expansion of the Hermes ecosystem by turning manual environment setups and development tasks into permanent, executable assets.
highlights: Automatically detects and proposes reusable skills from active workflows
Generates both SKILL.md documentation and Python plugin scaffolds
Provides slash commands to manage, list, and save detected patterns

ReinaMacCredy/maestro.summary: Maestro is a local-first conductor for multi-agent software engineering that enables persistent state management across separate agent sessions. It uses a CLI to store missions, features, and memory in a local .maestro directory, removing the need for external databases or servers. The tool allows human operators to coordinate multiple terminals by providing structured handoff briefs and cross-feature learning. This approach ensures that project context and agent progress remain disciplined and inspectable through a dedicated Mission Control TUI.
highlights: Local-first state management using on-disk .maestro directory and JSON snapshots
Structured mission planning with milestones, features, and validation assertions
Native handoff launches that generate markdown briefs for fresh agent runs

tiann/execplan-skill.summary: ExecPlan is an Agent Skill designed to help AI coding agents autonomously manage complex, long-running implementation tasks. It utilizes a structured execution plan that allows users to review and approve the approach before implementation starts. The system tracks progress in real-time and provides rollback procedures for safe recovery. It is compatible with tools such as Claude Code, Cursor, and Goose.
highlights: Autonomous execution for long-running tasks
Real-time tracking of progress and decisions
Built-in rollback procedures for safe recovery

cablate/Agentic-MCP-Skill.summary: Agentic-MCP-Skill is an experimental MCP client designed to optimize token usage through a three-layer progressive disclosure pattern. It utilizes a socket-based daemon architecture to manage persistent connections to MCP servers rather than loading full tool definitions into the AI context. By loading metadata, tool lists, and specific schemas only when required, the system can reduce token consumption by up to 86% compared to standard loading methods. The project currently supports hot-reloading configurations and provides a CLI for interacting with servers like Playwright and Filesystem.
highlights: Three-layer lazy loading reduces AI context token consumption
Socket-based daemon maintains persistent MCP server connections
Supports hot-reloading server configurations without restarting the daemon

armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge.summary: Skill Forge is a development tool that converts source code, documentation, and developer discourse into verified instruction files for AI agents to prevent API hallucinations. It uses Abstract Syntax Tree (AST) extraction to map agent instructions directly to specific file paths and line numbers in the source repository. The system generates version-pinned skills compliant with the agentskills.io specification, ensuring agents use correct function signatures and parameter types. It supports automated workflows for creating, testing, and exporting skills from both open-source repositories and closed-source SaaS documentation.
highlights: Maps every AI instruction to specific source code line numbers
Generates agentskills.io-compliant markdown and JSON instruction sets
Supports version-pinned skill management for multiple library releases

amanning3390/hermeshub.summary: HermesHub is a curated skills registry and marketplace for the Hermes Agent ecosystem that provides a centralized platform for discovering and installing AI capabilities. The system utilizes an automated security scanner with over 65 rules to vet skills for threats like data exfiltration and prompt injection before they are listed. It supports the agentskills.io open standard and features a creator marketplace where developers can monetize skills using crypto-based payment protocols. Users can manage their agent's capabilities through a command-line interface for browsing, searching, and one-click installation.
highlights: Automated security scanning for data exfiltration and prompt injection
Supports x402 and Micropayment protocols for premium skill monetization
Agent-to-agent feedback system with proof-of-use reviews and trust scores

PederHP/skillsdotnet.summary: SkillsDotNet is a C# implementation of the agentskills.io specification that enables the distribution and discovery of agent capabilities via the Model Context Protocol (MCP). It uses a "context-as-progressive-disclosure" approach where agents initially load small frontmatter summaries and only fetch full instructions or supporting files on demand. The library integrates with Microsoft.Extensions.AI to provide a standardized tool for models to load skills during active chat sessions. It includes built-in support for discovering skills from common agent directories like Claude, Cursor, and Copilot.
highlights: Implements agentskills.io specification using the C# MCP SDK
Provides progressive disclosure of skill context to save tokens
Supports automatic skill discovery from popular agent vendor directories

vectorize-io/hindsight.summary: Hindsight is an agent memory system designed to help AI agents learn and evolve through long-term retention, recall, and reflection workflows. It utilizes biomimetic data structures to organize information similarly to human memory, moving beyond standard vector search or knowledge graphs. The system provides an LLM wrapper for automatic memory management and supports manual control via Python and Node.js SDKs. It is optimized for conversational AI and autonomous agents that require per-user personalization and behavioral adaptation based on feedback.
highlights: Implements retain, recall, and reflect workflows for long-term agent learning
Uses biomimetic data structures to organize facts and mental models
Integrates with major LLM providers including OpenAI, Anthropic, and Ollama

greyhaven-ai/autocontext.summary: autocontext is a recursive self-improving harness designed to help LLM agents succeed on complex tasks by turning repeated work into validated, reusable knowledge. It operates through a structured multi-agent loop where competitors propose strategies, analysts evaluate outcomes, and coaches update playbooks to ensure agents improve across generations. The system supports various execution surfaces including simulations, evidence-driven investigations, and verifier-driven missions. Users can eventually distill stable agent behaviors into cheaper local runtimes using exported training data.
highlights: Accumulates validated knowledge to improve agent performance across repeated runs
Multi-agent loop featuring competitor, analyst, coach, architect, and curator roles
Supports browser-integrated investigations and verifier-driven mission checkpoints

elkimek/honcho-self-hosted.summary: This project provides a configuration layer for self-hosting Plastic Labs' Honcho memory backend, enabling cross-session persistence for Hermes Agent without relying on third-party cloud storage. It deploys the full Honcho stack—including the API, PostgreSQL with pgvector, and Redis—on local hardware using Docker Compose. Users can route memory extraction and reasoning tasks through any OpenAI-compatible API or local inference servers like Ollama to maintain data sovereignty. This setup allows Hermes to build long-term user profiles and logical observations while keeping conversation data on the user's own infrastructure.
highlights: Enables private cross-session memory for Hermes Agent via self-hosting
Supports any OpenAI-compatible LLM provider or local inference servers
Includes automated setup script for Docker, PostgreSQL, and Redis

yoloshii/ClawMem.summary: ClawMem is a local-first memory and context engine designed to provide persistent knowledge across different AI agent runtimes. It utilizes a hybrid retrieval architecture that combines vector search, BM25, and multi-graph traversal to surface relevant project documentation and past decisions. The system automatically captures session insights using local observer models and maintains a unified SQLite vault shared between Claude Code, OpenClaw, and Hermes agents. By operating entirely on-device without cloud dependencies, it ensures data privacy while managing document lifecycles and causal relationships between facts.
highlights: Unified local SQLite vault for cross-agent memory persistence
Hybrid retrieval combining vector search, BM25, and graph traversal
Automated decision capture and handoff generation via local models

plur-ai/plur.summary: PLUR is a persistent, local-first memory layer designed to synchronize agent corrections, preferences, and conventions across different AI tools and sessions. It stores knowledge as plain YAML files called engrams, using an activation model that strengthens frequently used facts while allowing stale information to naturally decay. The system employs a hybrid search approach combining BM25 and embeddings to inject relevant context into agent prompts without cloud API calls. It integrates directly with Hermes Agent, Claude Code, and OpenClaw to automatically capture and recall learnings during active conversations.
highlights: Stores persistent agent memory locally in open YAML engram format
Uses hybrid BM25 and embedding search for zero-cost local retrieval
Automates memory injection and extraction for Hermes and MCP tools

amanning3390/flowstate-qmd.summary: FlowState-QMD is a local-first memory layer designed to provide coding agents with immediate project context from markdown documentation. It utilizes a SQLite-based vector store and an anticipatory cache that prefetches relevant documents by monitoring active agent session logs. This approach allows agents to access design docs, ADRs, and changelogs before initiating a reactive search loop. The system integrates via an MCP server and supports local execution using GGUF models and node-llama-cpp.
highlights: Anticipatory cache prefetches context by watching agent session logs
Local-first storage using SQLite-vec and GGUF models via node-llama-cpp
MCP-native server with automated configuration for major coding agent clients

robbyczgw-cla/hermes-web-search-plus.summary: Hermes Web Search Plus is a multi-provider search plugin for the Hermes Agent that expands information retrieval beyond standard web tools. It utilizes an intelligent auto-routing engine to select the most appropriate search provider based on query intent, such as using Exa for research or Serper for news. The system features adaptive fallback logic to skip providers on cooldown and includes local caching to minimize redundant API calls. Users can also perform deep document analysis and filter results by time range or specific domains.
highlights: Supports eight providers including Serper, Brave, Tavily, and Exa
Intelligent auto-routing based on query intent and linguistic patterns
Advanced filtering for time ranges and domain whitelisting or blacklisting

42-evey/hermes-plugins.summary: Evey's Hermes Plugins is a collection of 23 custom extensions designed to enhance the autonomy, observability, and cost-efficiency of the Hermes Agent framework. The suite implements specialized logic for multi-model decision debates, smart task routing with fallback chains, and structured telemetry logging. These tools enable agents to manage their own goals, perform self-correction loops, and consolidate long-term memory into vector storage. Additionally, the project includes a bidirectional bridge for Claude Code and budget enforcement via Langfuse integration.
highlights: Autonomous planning, reflection, and multi-model consensus debate tools
Smart model routing with sensitivity filters and four-model fallback chains
Automated memory consolidation into Qdrant vectors and identity evolution

FahrenheitResearch/hermes-weather-plugin.summary: The Hermes Weather Plugin provides a suite of 13 meteorological tools for the Hermes Agent ecosystem, enabling real-time access to observations, forecasts, and high-fidelity model imagery. It utilizes a Python-first architecture that offloads heavy computational tasks, such as NEXRAD radar rendering and ECAPE calculations, to high-performance Rust backends. The plugin features an automated bootstrap process that compiles necessary Rust binaries on first use, ensuring a streamlined setup for complex weather analysis. Users can retrieve verified model soundings, severe weather outlooks from the SPC, and global data via Open-Meteo integration.
highlights: Thirteen tools for NWS forecasts, radar imagery, and severe weather alerts
Rust-backed meteorological calculations for ECAPE, soundings, and parcel profiles
Automated bootstrap system for compiling and caching required Rust binaries

42-evey/evey-bridge-plugin.summary: The Evey Bridge Plugin is a technical integration that connects Claude Code with the Evey hermes-agent stack. It functions by automatically checking for messages or tasks from the bridge and injecting them as context into every prompt submission. The plugin includes specialized commands for running a 'Mother Mode' improvement loop and monitoring system health, including wallet and cron status. It is designed specifically for users running the Evey stack at a designated local directory.
highlights: Injects Evey messages and tasks into Claude Code prompts
Automates Mother Mode improvement loops via the /evey-loop command
Monitors health, wallets, and crons with quick status checks

nativ3ai/hermes-payguard.summary: Hermes PayGuard is a security-focused plugin that enables Hermes Agents to handle USDC and x402 payments through a gated execution model. It separates payment preparation from execution by requiring an out-of-band human approval stamp for transfers exceeding user-defined policy limits. The system supports Circle developer-controlled wallets, cross-chain CCTP transfers, and automated x402 micropayments for paid HTTP fetches. This architecture ensures that while agents can stage financial intents, they cannot unilaterally move significant funds without explicit operator consent.
highlights: Enforces human-in-the-loop approval for high-value USDC transfers
Supports Circle CCTP for cross-chain USDC routing and execution
Automates x402 micropayments below configurable policy thresholds

raulvidis/hermes-cloudflare.summary: This plugin integrates Cloudflare's Browser Rendering API into the Hermes Agent ecosystem to enable web crawling and content extraction from JavaScript-heavy websites. It utilizes Cloudflare's headless browser infrastructure to perform tasks like CSS-selector scraping, PDF generation, and full-page screenshot capture. The tool also features AI-powered structured data extraction using Llama 3.3 70B via Workers AI. Users can automate complex web workflows including sitemap discovery and Markdown conversion directly through agent commands.
highlights: Crawls websites with depth controls and sitemap discovery support
Extracts structured JSON data using Workers AI and Llama 3.3
Converts fully rendered JavaScript-heavy pages into clean Markdown

builderz-labs/mission-control.summary: Mission Control is a self-hosted dashboard designed to orchestrate and monitor fleets of AI agents. It utilizes a local-first architecture powered by SQLite and Next.js to coordinate multi-agent workflows, dispatch tasks, and track operational costs without external database dependencies. The platform features built-in quality gates for task approval, real-time telemetry via WebSockets, and a security framework that includes secret detection and trust scoring. It integrates with various agent frameworks like CrewAI and LangGraph, and provides a bridge for CLI tools such as Claude Code.
highlights: Self-hosted orchestration using SQLite with zero external dependencies
Multi-gateway support for CrewAI, LangGraph, AutoGen, and Claude SDK
Built-in Aegis review system for human-in-the-loop task sign-off

swarmclawai/swarmclaw.summary: SwarmClaw is a self-hosted runtime designed to manage and orchestrate autonomous AI agent swarms. It functions as an execution environment that supports memory, scheduling, delegation, and runtime skills across various model providers. The platform integrates with OpenClaw gateways and supports MCP-native workflows to facilitate complex multi-agent coordination. Users can visualize agent teams through an organizational chart and manage interactions via a durable chat interface.
highlights: Supports 23 built-in model and CLI providers
Features memory, delegation, and scheduled agent tasks
Provides visual org charts for agent team management

howdymary/hermes-agent-metaharness.summary: Hermes Agent Meta-Harness is a standalone outer-loop optimization framework designed to improve the performance of Hermes agents on verifiable coding benchmarks. It treats the agent as an execution backend and searches for optimal harness configurations—the code governing context collection and retrieval—rather than modifying model weights. The system automates benchmark orchestration, archive analysis, and frontier tracking to identify high-performing harness candidates. It currently supports TBLite and TB2 benchmarks, providing structured mutation and search capabilities to refine agentic workflows.
highlights: Optimizes harness code through an automated outer-loop search process
Orchestrates TBLite and TB2 benchmark evaluation via Hermes Agent
Tracks performance frontiers with structured candidate mutation and comparison

runtimenoteslabs/gladiator.summary: Gladiator is an autonomous agent arena that pits two AI companies against each other to compete for GitHub stars on identical repositories. The system uses Paperclip for orchestration and Hermes Agent workers to execute tasks according to distinct corporate strategies: growth-focused Blitz versus quality-focused Craft. A real-time dashboard visualizes the competition through scoreboards, task boards, and audit trails. Upon completion, the project demonstrates cross-company skill transfer by merging the rival entities and their learned memories.
highlights: Orchestrates 9 autonomous agents across two competing AI companies
Uses Paperclip and Hermes Agent for task execution and learning
Real-time dashboard visualizes agent tasks, code, and scoreboard metrics

1ilkhamov/opencode-hermes-multiagent.summary: Hermes is a multi-agent orchestration system built for OpenCode AI to manage the full software development lifecycle. It utilizes a master orchestrator to route tasks through a pipeline of 17 specialized agents categorized into research, planning, implementation, quality, documentation, and infrastructure roles. The system enforces mandatory quality gates and security audits while maintaining context across different LLM models including GPT, Gemini, and Claude. It supports automated workflows for feature development, bug fixing, refactoring, and performance optimization.
highlights: Coordinates 17 specialized agents across distinct development phases
Implements mandatory quality gates for code reviews and testing
Integrates multiple LLM providers including OpenAI, Anthropic, and Google

supermodeltools/bigiron.summary: Big Iron is an experimental AI-native software development system that automates the full development lifecycle through a graph-gated workflow. It integrates Hermes Agent with the Supermodel Code Graph API to replace traditional file-loading context with structural intelligence, including call graphs and dependency maps. The system executes an eight-phase process—from planning to autonomous health checks—using specific skills and quality gates to ensure architectural alignment. While now deprecated in favor of the Supermodel CLI, it serves as a prototype for autonomous, graph-grounded code generation and refactoring.
highlights: Executes an autonomous eight-phase SDLC from planning to health checks
Uses Supermodel MCP to provide Hermes with structural code graphs
Enforces architectural quality through automated graph-diff phase gates

Rainhoole/hermes-agent-acp-skill.summary: The hermes-acp-orchestrator is a skill designed to standardize multi-agent delegation within engineering workflows. It functions by routing specific goals to specialized agents including Hermes subagents, Codex, and Claude Code while maintaining strict context isolation. The system ensures operational stability through configurable safety controls such as execution timeouts and output character limits. This tool is primarily intended for complex tasks requiring distinct phases like implementation, risk analysis, and final synthesis.
highlights: Routes tasks between Hermes subagents, Codex, and Claude Code.
Prevents parent context pollution through isolated delegation workflows.
Enforces safety via configurable timeouts and output character limits.

numtide/llm-agents.nix.summary: llm-agents.nix provides a collection of Nix packages designed to streamline the installation and management of AI coding agents and development tools. It offers a centralized repository of packages that are automatically updated on a daily basis. Users can easily deploy a variety of terminal-based agents, orchestrators, and proxy APIs for diverse AI providers. This ecosystem supports diverse workflows, ranging from local agent execution to multi-agent coordination.
highlights: Daily automatic updates for all included packages
Supports diverse AI agents like Claude Code and Goose
Includes multi-agent orchestrators and unified proxy APIs

TheAiSingularity/hermesclaw.summary: HermesClaw is a deployment framework that runs the Hermes Agent within NVIDIA’s OpenShell sandbox to provide hardware-level security enforcement. It utilizes kernel-level mechanisms like Landlock LSM and Seccomp BPF to restrict network egress, filesystem access, and system calls. The environment supports persistent memory, self-improving skills, and multi-agent delegation while maintaining strict isolation from the host OS. Users can toggle between security policy presets to enable or disable external integrations like Telegram, Discord, and web searching.
highlights: Hardware-enforced sandboxing via NVIDIA OpenShell and kernel-level syscall blocking
Pre-configured security policies ranging from strict inference to permissive access
Integrated library of 40+ tools including terminal, vision, and messaging

0xrsydn/nix-hermes-agent.summary: nix-hermes-agent provides a Nix package and NixOS module for the declarative deployment of Hermes Agent. It allows users to manage the entire agent lifecycle—including configuration, workspace documents, and systemd services—through a single Nix configuration file. The module ensures reproducibility by rendering settings into a structured CLI configuration while keeping sensitive API keys secure via external environment files. It supports both Nix-managed declarative skills and native Hermes CLI interactive skills, providing a stable foundation for production-ready agent infrastructure.
highlights: Declarative management of agent config, documents, and systemd services
Secure secrets handling via systemd EnvironmentFiles and sops-nix integration
Hybrid skill support for both Nix-managed and interactive CLI installs

rookiemann/portable-hermes-agent.summary: Portable Hermes Agent is a Windows desktop implementation of the Hermes Agent designed for non-technical users to run AI agents without installation or admin rights. It utilizes a Tkinter GUI and an embedded Python environment to bridge local models via LM Studio or cloud providers like OpenRouter. The system features a workflow engine for automating multi-step pipelines and a dynamic tool maker for creating custom API wrappers at runtime. It also integrates portable servers for text-to-speech, music generation, and ComfyUI image generation.
highlights: 100 tools including local model management and GPU monitoring
No-install deployment requiring no admin rights or Docker
Integrated workflow engine for scheduled and conditional automation

xmbshwll/hermes-agent-docker.summary: This project provides a minimal Docker sandbox image designed to package and deploy Hermes Agent. It utilizes the official upstream installation script to set up the agent and includes mini-swe-agent within the container. The image supports multi-arch publishing and allows for persistent state management by mounting the agent's home directory. Users can easily build specific versions using build arguments to target different Hermes branches or tags.
highlights: Includes mini-swe-agent and official Hermes installation
Supports persistent state via mounted home directories
Allows building specific Hermes tags using build arguments

JackTheGit/hermes-autonomous-server.summary: This project provides a deployment framework for running Hermes Agent as a persistent, headless Linux service. It utilizes systemd to manage the Hermes Gateway, ensuring the agent remains active across reboots without requiring an open terminal session. The setup integrates with the Nous Portal for model access and leverages the native Hermes cron scheduler to automate recurring AI tasks. This approach offers a stable, self-hosted alternative to manual shell loops or third-party hosting for autonomous server operations.
highlights: Configures Hermes Gateway as a reboot-safe systemd service
Enables autonomous execution of recurring AI cron jobs
Supports headless operation on Ubuntu and Debian servers

ellickjohnson/portainer-stack-hermes.summary: This project provides a Docker Compose configuration for deploying the Hermes agent alongside a ttyd web terminal. It utilizes a pre-built image from the GitHub Container Registry to facilitate browser-based terminal access to the agent. Users can manage the deployment as a Git-based stack within the Portainer UI for simplified container orchestration. The setup includes an automated GitHub Action workflow for building and updating the underlying container image.
highlights: Deploys Hermes agent with a ttyd web terminal interface
Integrates directly with Portainer as a Git Repository stack
Automates image builds using GitHub Actions and GHCR

raulvidis/hermes-android.summary: Hermes-android is a remote automation bridge that enables Hermes agents to control Android devices through a Kotlin-based mobile app and a Python toolset. The system establishes an outbound WebSocket connection from the phone to a server, bypassing the need for port forwarding or USB cables via a 6-character pairing code. It provides 36 specialized tools for UI interaction, including screen reading, gesture execution, and system-level actions like making calls or sending SMS. This integration allows agents to perform autonomous mobile tasks such as checking notifications, managing contacts, and navigating third-party applications.
highlights: Executes 36 tools for UI navigation, media control, and system actions
Uses AccessibilityService for screen reading and coordinate-based gesture automation
Connects via WebSocket relay to work behind NAT and firewalls

teknium1/hermes-miniverse.summary: Hermes-miniverse is a bridge that enables Hermes Agents to inhabit and interact within Miniverse pixel worlds. It functions through a three-part architecture consisting of a standalone bridge daemon, a gateway hook for state broadcasting, and a specific skill that allows the agent to consciously navigate the environment. This integration allows agents to maintain a visual presence, update their status in real-time, and engage in bidirectional communication with other AI entities. Users can deploy multiple bridges simultaneously to populate a virtual world with specialized Hermes instances.
highlights: Connects Hermes Agents to Miniverse pixel-based virtual environments
Synchronizes live agent states including working, thinking, and idle
Enables bidirectional messaging between Hermes and other world agents

Ridwannurudeen/hermes-council.summary: Hermes-council is an MCP server designed to provide adversarial, multi-perspective decision-making for the hermes-agent ecosystem. It functions by orchestrating five distinct personas—Advocate, Skeptic, Oracle, Contrarian, and Arbiter—to debate complex questions through structured deliberation. The system produces calibrated verdicts with confidence scores and evidence links, and it can be used to generate DPO preference pairs for Reinforcement Learning training.
highlights: Five distinct intellectual personas for adversarial deliberation
Generates DPO preference pairs for RL training
Supports custom persona configuration via YAML

marlandoj/zouroboros-swarm-executors.summary: Zouroboros Swarm Executors is a bridge system that integrates local CLI-based AI tools into the Zo Computer swarm orchestration ecosystem. It functions by using bash bridge scripts to wrap tools like Hermes, Claude Code, and Gemini, allowing them to receive tasks via standard input and return results via standard output. This approach enables local execution of complex tasks such as security audits and web research while maintaining a shared identity and memory system across all personas. The project includes a health-check system to validate environment configurations and a registry for adding custom CLI tools as swarm members.
highlights: Wraps CLI tools into AI personas with shared identity files
Provides bridge scripts for Hermes, Claude Code, Gemini, and Codex
Routes tasks locally instead of through remote API calls

gizdusum/hermes-blockchain-oracle.summary: Hermes Blockchain Oracle is a Model Context Protocol (MCP) server that provides Hermes Agent with real-time access to the Solana blockchain. It functions as a bridge between natural language queries and on-chain data by exposing specialized tools for wallet, token, and transaction analysis. The server enables users to monitor network health, track whale movements, and manage NFT portfolios through conversational interactions. By integrating directly with Solana RPC nodes, it eliminates the need for manual block explorer searches.
highlights: Provides real-time Solana wallet balances and token portfolio tracking
Detects large-scale whale transfers with configurable movement thresholds
Retrieves NFT metadata, collection details, and floor price information

junhoyeo/tokscale.summary: Tokscale is a high-performance CLI tool and visualization dashboard designed to track token consumption and costs across various AI coding agents. It works by scanning local session databases and logs from supported clients to aggregate usage data into a native Rust TUI or a web-based 3D contribution graph. The tool integrates with LiteLLM pricing data to provide real-time cost calculations, including tiered pricing and cache discounts. It supports a wide range of agents including Hermes Agent, Claude Code, OpenClaw, and Cursor.
highlights: Tracks token usage across 20+ AI coding agents and CLIs
Provides real-time cost calculations using LiteLLM pricing data
Features a native Rust TUI and 3D visualization dashboard

joeynyc/hermes-skins.summary: Hermes Skins is a collection of visual themes for the Hermes CLI agent that allows users to customize the terminal's appearance. It uses YAML configuration files to modify elements like banner colors, ASCII art, spinner verbs, and branding text without altering the agent's behavior. Users can apply these skins via session commands or permanent configuration files. The project includes a wide variety of themed presets ranging from retro computing aesthetics to pop-culture references.
highlights: Customizes colors, ASCII art, and spinner verbs via YAML
Supports partial skins that inherit values from the default theme
Includes numerous themed presets like Vault-Tec, Skynet, and DOS

AlexAI-MCP/hermes-CCC.summary: hermes-CCC is a port of the Hermes Agent framework specifically designed for the Claude Code native skill system. It integrates the Hermes brain architecture directly into the Claude Code execution context, eliminating the need for external processes or OAuth authentication. The project implements 46 core skills across categories such as MLOps, software development, and research, including specialized tools for RL training and vector database management. This allows developers to utilize Hermes-style memory, personas, and task routing while remaining within a unified terminal environment.
highlights: Port of Hermes Agent brain and 46 core skills to CCC
Runs natively in Claude Code without separate processes or OAuth
Includes MLOps tools for vLLM, GGUF inference, and RL training

unmodeled-tyler/vessel-browser.summary: Vessel is an open-source Chromium-based browser designed to serve as a persistent runtime for autonomous agents rather than human-only navigation. It operates via the Model Context Protocol (MCP) to provide external agent harnesses with durable state, named sessions, and structured page visibility. The interface includes a supervisory UI that allows humans to audit, intervene, and redirect long-running agent workflows in real-time. It integrates with Hermes Agent and other MCP clients to transform web automation from a headless process into a transparent, steerable environment.
highlights: Provides durable browser state with named sessions and checkpoints
Exposes structured page schemas and element visibility via MCP
Features a supervisory sidebar for human intervention and workflow auditing

Abruptive/Ankh.md.summary: Ankh.md is an open-source framework designed to transform a single Hermes Agent into multiple specialized, project-specific instances. It works by detecting a local `.agent/` directory and dynamically overriding the default `hermes` command with a scoped agent configuration. This allows developers to maintain unique instructions, skills, and memories for different codebases or workflows without cross-project interference.
highlights: Per-folder scoping with local configuration and skills
Seamlessly switches between default and scoped Hermes agents
Supports project-local memories, tools, and custom instructions

roli-lpci/lintlang.summary: lintlang is a static analysis tool designed to identify language-level failures in AI agent configurations, system prompts, and tool definitions. It employs seven structural detectors to catch issues like ambiguous tool descriptions and missing termination conditions without making any LLM calls. By running locally or in CI pipelines, the tool provides immediate feedback and concrete fix suggestions to prevent agent loops and incorrect tool selection. It supports multiple file formats including YAML, JSON, and Markdown, ensuring prompt quality before runtime testing.
highlights: Identifies structural language bugs using seven specialized static detectors.
Operates with zero LLM calls, zero latency, and no API keys.
Provides automated quality gating for CI/CD pipelines with verdict-based exits.

0xNyk/openclaw-to-hermes.summary: openclaw-to-hermes is a migration utility designed to transition users from OpenClaw to Hermes Agent. It automates the transfer of agent personas, workspace files, model configurations, and credentials between the two ecosystems. The tool addresses specific limitations found in earlier migration methods, such as incorrect authentication formatting and incomplete workspace detection. It provides a safe migration path through dry-run modes and automated backup creation.
highlights: Migrates agent personas, memories, skills, and workspace files
Maps complex OpenClaw model configurations to Hermes YAML format
Handles credential transfers from local and system-level environment files

42-evey/evey-setup.summary: Evey Setup is a deployment toolkit designed to bootstrap a complete autonomous AI agent environment based on the Hermes Agent framework. It uses a multi-phase shell script to orchestrate a containerized stack including model routing via LiteLLM, local GPU inference through Ollama, and vector memory with Qdrant. The installer provides three service tiers—Base, Services, and Full—allowing users to scale from a minimal agent to a production-ready system with monitoring and workflow automation. It integrates 29 community plugins and pre-configures free model routing to enable zero-cost operation.
highlights: Deploys Hermes Agent with LiteLLM, Ollama, and Qdrant via Docker
Includes 29 community plugins for memory, social, and quality validation
Automates configuration for free model routing and local GPU inference

ucsandman/DashClaw.summary: DashClaw is a decision governance infrastructure designed to intercept and evaluate agent actions before they execute. It functions as a middleware layer between AI agents and external systems, enforcing guard policies and conducting risk assessments to prevent destructive actions. The platform provides verifiable evidence of every decision and supports human-in-the-loop approvals via Discord. It integrates with major frameworks like LangChain, CrewAI, and Claude Code through MCP servers, SDKs, or zero-code hooks.
highlights: Intercepts agent tool calls to enforce safety and guard policies
Provides real-time risk assessment and Discord-based manual approval workflows
Records verifiable evidence and audit trails for every agent decision

Lethe044/hermes-incident-commander.summary: Hermes Incident Commander is an autonomous SRE agent designed to automate the detection, diagnosis, and remediation of production infrastructure failures. It utilizes the Hermes Agent framework to monitor system vitals, classify incident severity, and execute multi-step diagnostic pipelines to identify root causes. Once a fix is applied and verified, the agent generates structured post-mortem reports and creates new prevention skills to handle similar future incidents more efficiently. The system integrates with Telegram and Discord for real-time alerting and supports cloud-native remediation via MCP servers.
highlights: Automates incident triage, root cause analysis, and remediation actions.
Generates persistent prevention skills and post-incident reports autonomously.
Supports parallel subagent spawning for multi-layer service investigations.

Christabel337/job-scout-agent.summary: Job Scout Agent is an automated job search tool designed to streamline the discovery and tracking of employment opportunities. The agent utilizes Hermes Agent capabilities to fetch live listings from job board APIs, score them against user-defined profiles, and perform company research. It organizes findings into structured local directories and maintains a CSV tracker for easy integration with Google Sheets. This system eliminates manual searching by providing a daily summary of relevant roles with direct application links.
highlights: Fetches live job listings using browser-based API integration
Scores roles against skills, salary, and location preferences
Maintains an automated CSV tracker for application management

rodmarkun/anihermes.summary: AniHermes is a natural language anime media server and tracker built on the Hermes Agent framework. It leverages LLM orchestration to manage downloads via qBittorrent, track series progress, and organize local libraries through conversational interfaces like Telegram, Discord, or CLI. The system utilizes agentic capabilities such as vision analysis for identifying anime from screenshots and automated CRON jobs for scheduling weekly episode downloads. It integrates directly with Anilist for watchlist synchronization and provides parallel search functionality across multiple sources like SubsPlease and Nyaa.
highlights: Natural language control for downloads, tracking, and library management
Vision-based anime identification from screenshots using Hermes Agent
Automated weekly episode tracking via NL-defined CRON jobs

JackTheGit/hermes-ai-infrastructure-moni.summary: This project is a reference implementation for building autonomous infrastructure monitoring systems using Hermes Agent. It utilizes cron-based workflows to ingest AI research from arXiv and track trending infrastructure repositories on GitHub. The system automatically generates structured reports on compute risk levels, infrastructure trends, and cost projections. It serves as a template for developers to deploy long-running, headless agentic systems for operational intelligence.
highlights: Automated research ingestion via arXiv API and GitHub ecosystem monitoring
Scheduled cron workflows for risk analysis and cost modeling
Generates markdown-based dashboards and structured infrastructure intelligence reports

bigph00t/hermescraft.summary: HermesCraft is an embodied AI framework that integrates Hermes agents into Minecraft as persistent, interactive players. It connects the Hermes agent stack to a Mineflayer bot body through a custom Minecraft CLI and HTTP API, allowing agents to perceive and interact with the game world. Agents can function as individual companions or as part of multi-agent civilizations with private messaging and social memory. The system emphasizes fair-play perception by restricting agent knowledge to line-of-sight visuals and directional audio rather than using privileged game data.
highlights: Embodied agents with persistent memory, identity, and tool-use capabilities
Fair-play perception system using line-of-sight filtering and spatial mapping
Multi-agent social features including public chat and private messaging

hxsteric/mercury.summary: Mercury is a blockchain cash flow analyzer and Hermes Agent skill designed to provide transparency into wallet activities across Ethereum, Base, and Solana. It utilizes parallel subagents to fetch transaction data, identify six specific fraud patterns, and generate AI-driven trading strategies based on wallet behavior. The project features a multi-layer WebGL and Canvas dashboard for real-time visualization of inflows, outflows, and risk metrics. It operates as a standalone tool or an integrated Hermes skill for automated multi-chain investigations.
highlights: Analyzes transaction flows across Ethereum, Base, and Solana chains
Detects six fraud patterns including mixer interactions and dust attacks
Visualizes data via interactive WebGL and Canvas-based dashboards

bryercowan/hermes-embodied.summary: Hermes Embodied is a robotics extension for Hermes Agent that enables autonomous fine-tuning of Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models through natural language. The system orchestrates a self-improvement loop by collecting robot trajectories in simulation or hardware, provisioning cloud GPUs via Vast.ai, and retraining models when performance thresholds are met. It integrates with the LeRobot framework and MuJoCo simulation to automate the transition from data collection to model promotion. This allows users to manage complex robotics training pipelines without manual machine learning expertise.
highlights: Automates VLA fine-tuning loops using natural language commands
Provisions and manages Vast.ai cloud GPU instances autonomously
Supports SmolVLA and GR00T models for simulation or physical hardware

0xNyk/awesome-hermes-agent.summary: This repository is a curated collection of resources, tools, and community-developed skills designed for the Hermes Agent ecosystem. It organizes the landscape of the self-improving AI agent by categorizing official Nous Research projects, third-party skill libraries, and deployment utilities. Users can find specialized integrations for messaging platforms like Telegram and Discord, as well as meta-skills that allow the agent to auto-generate its own capabilities from workflows. The list serves as a central hub for developers to extend the agent's memory, reasoning, and task-execution layers.
highlights: Tracks official Nous Research core repositories and documentation
Catalogs community skills for goal management and literate programming
Lists multi-platform bridges for Telegram, Discord, and Slack

alchaincyf/hermes-agent-orange-book.summary: The Hermes Agent Orange Book is a practical guide designed to help users master the Hermes Agent open-source AI framework. It explains the framework's technical architecture, including its three-layer memory system, self-improving learning loop, and automatic skill evolution. The guide provides 17 chapters of content covering installation, customization, and real-world applications such as development automation and knowledge assistants.
highlights: Detailed guides on memory systems and learning loops
Step-by-step setup and multi-platform customization instructions
Practical scenarios for dev automation and multi-agent systems

OnlyTerp/hermes-optimization-guide.summary: The Hermes Optimization Guide is a comprehensive technical resource and toolkit designed to move Hermes Agent deployments from basic installation to production-ready environments. It provides a 21-part curriculum alongside runnable artifacts, including a one-command VPS bootstrap script, hardened systemd units, and pre-configured Caddy templates for secure reverse proxying. Users can implement opinionated configurations for cost-optimization, security hardening, and multi-platform integration across 16 different communication gateways. The project also includes 13 installable skills and reference architectures for scaling from local homelabs to professional agency deployments.
highlights: One-command VPS bootstrap script for automated production-ready deployment.
Thirteen installable skills and five pre-tuned configuration templates.
Reference architectures for integrating MCP servers and remote sandboxes.

metantonio/hermes-wsl-ubuntu.summary: This project provides a comprehensive setup guide and automation script for running Hermes Agent locally on Windows via WSL2, Linux, or macOS. It integrates llama.cpp for model inference with Qwen3.5 GGUF models, utilizing CUDA or Metal acceleration for optimized performance. The guide covers the full stack including the Camofox browser server for web-browsing tasks and the Hermes HUDUI for a graphical user interface. Users can deploy a production-ready agentic environment with local LLM support and OpenAI-compatible API endpoints.
highlights: Automated installation script for Hermes Agent, llama.cpp, and Camofox
Supports GPU acceleration via CUDA for WSL2 and Metal for macOS
Configures Qwen3.5 GGUF models for local inference and web browsing

mudrii/hermes-agent-docs.summary: Hermes Agent is an autonomous AI system designed for persistent learning and multi-platform deployment across various messaging and development environments. It utilizes a closed learning loop to create reusable skills from experience, manage long-term memory via SQLite, and maintain a consistent user model across sessions. The agent supports concurrent tool execution and integrates with dozens of model providers and messaging gateways, including Telegram, Discord, and Slack. Its architecture allows for extensible functionality through a plugin system and Model Context Protocol (MCP) server management.
highlights: Autonomous skill creation and self-improvement based on task performance
Unified messaging gateway supporting over 15 communication platforms
Multi-provider flexibility with support for Nous Portal, Anthropic, and OpenAI

martymcenroe/HermesWiki.summary: HermesWiki is a centralized documentation repository designed to support users of the Hermes autonomous AI email agent. It provides a structured knowledge base that outlines deployment patterns and practical implementation recipes for the agent. By consolidating technical guidance, the project helps developers configure and optimize their AI-driven email workflows. These resources ensure that users can effectively navigate the setup and operational requirements of the Hermes ecosystem.
highlights: Centralized documentation for the Hermes autonomous email agent
Provides specific deployment patterns for technical implementation
Contains practical recipes for configuring AI email workflows

nativ3ai/hermes-agent-camel.summary: This project is a specialized fork of Hermes Agent that integrates CaMeL trust-boundary principles to defend against prompt-injection attacks during autonomous execution. It implements a security layer that monitors or enforces policies to block unauthorized sensitive actions when the agent is operating under untrusted contexts. Users can explicitly opt into these protections using specific CLI flags while maintaining access to the core Hermes features like autonomous skill creation and multi-platform messaging. The system supports various runtime modes, including a monitor mode for tracing policy decisions and an enforcement mode for active threat mitigation.
highlights: Implements CaMeL trust-boundary principles for prompt-injection defense
Offers monitor and enforce modes for granular security control
Maintains full compatibility with upstream Hermes Agent features

kaminocorp/hermes-alpha.summary: Hermes Alpha is an experimental framework designed to test if a stock AI agent can autonomously bootstrap and manage a bug bounty system from first principles. The architecture utilizes a two-agent loop where a persistent 'Overseer' agent builds, deploys, and refines an ephemeral 'Hunter' agent tasked with finding software vulnerabilities. By providing the agent with only a Linux terminal and an identity document rather than custom tools, the project evaluates the self-improvement capabilities of the Hermes model. The system integrates with Fly.io for managed infrastructure and uses Elephantasm for long-term memory streams.
highlights: Autonomous two-agent loop for self-improving vulnerability research
Web-based terminal interface with persistent WebSocket sessions
Managed deployment of sub-agents via Fly.io infrastructure

beardthelion/hermes-skill-distillation.summary: This project provides a skill distillation pipeline designed to fine-tune Hermes 4 models using real-world agentic trajectories. It captures tool-use sessions from the Hermes agent and converts them into high-quality training data through a specialized environment and automated scoring system. The pipeline supports both offline SFT data generation and live reinforcement learning via integration with the Atropos training framework. By focusing on grounded tasks like coding and system administration, it enables a closed learning loop where agent performance directly informs model improvements.
highlights: Generates SFT-ready JSONL data from 30 diverse real-world task categories
Automates trajectory scoring based on completion, efficiency, and error recovery
Integrates with Atropos for live reinforcement learning and model evaluation

xaspx/hermes-control-interface.summary: The Hermes Control Interface is a self-hosted web dashboard designed to manage the Hermes AI agent stack through a centralized graphical interface. It utilizes a Node.js and Express backend with WebSockets to provide real-time terminal access, file management, and multi-agent gateway control. Users can monitor system metrics, track token usage analytics, and configure agent skills or cron jobs without using the command line. The platform includes a revamped chat interface with tool call visualization and a robust role-based access control system for team management.
highlights: Multi-agent gateway for starting, stopping, and configuring multiple profiles.
Real-time chat interface with session management and tool call cards.
Detailed token analytics tracking costs by model, platform, and time.

ksimback/hermes-ecosystem.summary: Hermes Atlas is a community-curated directory and ecosystem map for the Hermes Agent by Nous Research. It aggregates over 80 quality-filtered repositories, including skills, plugins, and deployment templates, using a vanilla JavaScript frontend and Vercel serverless functions. The platform features a RAG-powered chatbot that answers technical questions grounded in 27 research files using hybrid retrieval and OpenRouter LLMs. Users can search and filter the ecosystem while viewing live GitHub star counts and growth trends cached via Redis.
highlights: Directory of 80+ security-reviewed Hermes Agent tools and integrations
RAG chatbot providing grounded answers from 27 research files
Live GitHub star tracking with Redis-cached sparklines and trends

dodo-reach/hermes-desktop.summary: Hermes Desktop is a native macOS application that provides a dedicated workspace for managing Hermes Agent hosts via SSH. It connects directly to remote machines to manage sessions, skills, and cron jobs without requiring a gateway API, local file mirroring, or remote daemon. The app features a multi-tabbed terminal, conflict-aware file editing, and profile-specific usage analytics. It serves as a local companion for users who want a polished desktop interface while maintaining the remote host as the single source of truth.
highlights: Direct SSH connection with no middle-layer API or remote daemon
Real multi-tabbed terminal with profile-aware environment variables
Management of remote cron jobs, skills, and SQLite session stores

clawshell/clawshell.summary: ClawShell is a security-privileged process that acts as a safety harness for the Hermes Agent ecosystem by protecting sensitive credentials and PII. It functions as a proxy between the agent and upstream LLM providers, swapping virtual keys for real ones stored in a restricted directory to ensure the agent never has direct access to credentials. The system performs Data Loss Prevention (DLP) scanning to redact or block sensitive information in request and response bodies. Additionally, it provides secure email isolation through IMAP sender filtering and supports OAuth-based authentication for providers like OpenAI.
highlights: Maps virtual keys to real credentials stored in protected Unix directories
Scans and redacts PII in request and response bodies via regex
Enforces sender-based email filtering for secure mailbox access

nesquena/hermes-webui.summary: Hermes WebUI provides a lightweight, browser-based interface for the Hermes Agent, offering a visual alternative to the standard command-line environment. Built with Python and vanilla JavaScript, the application maintains full parity with the CLI without requiring complex build steps or frameworks. The interface features a three-panel layout for managing chat sessions, workspace files, and agent controls like model selection and token usage monitoring. It allows users to interact with their agent's persistent memory, self-improving skills, and scheduled jobs through a secure web or mobile-friendly portal.
highlights: Full feature parity with the Hermes Agent CLI experience
Three-panel layout for chat, file browsing, and session management
Zero-build architecture using Python and vanilla JavaScript

garrytan/gbrain.summary: GBrain is an opinionated memory and context layer designed to provide AI agents with a persistent, self-wiring knowledge graph. It utilizes a hybrid search architecture and automated entity extraction to link people, companies, and concepts without requiring constant LLM calls. The system ingests diverse data sources like meetings, emails, and tweets to build a structured timeline and backlink-boosted ranking system. It integrates with Hermes Agent and OpenClaw, offering over 28 specialized skills for autonomous memory maintenance and information retrieval.
highlights: Self-wiring knowledge graph with automated entity and relationship extraction.
Hybrid search combining vector-only RAG with graph-based ranking.
Ships with 28 specialized skills for autonomous ingestion and enrichment.

pyrate-llama/hermes-ui.summary: Hermes UI is a glassmorphic web interface designed for the self-hosted Hermes Agent AI assistant. It operates as a single-file HTML application built with React 18, utilizing a lightweight Python proxy server to handle real-time communication and file management. The interface features a multi-functional dashboard for chat, file browsing, and memory inspection, while providing live log streaming via Server-Sent Events. Users can manage MCP tools, execute cron jobs, and preview generated artifacts like HTML or SVG directly within a dedicated side panel.
highlights: Single-file HTML architecture using React 18 and Python proxy
Real-time artifact panel for live HTML, SVG, and PDF previews
Integrated file browser and memory inspector for local agent data

kevinmarmstrong/hermes-claude-code-rc.summary: This Hermes Agent plugin enables remote management of Claude Code sessions through a Telegram interface. It bridges the gap between mobile devices and local filesystems by allowing users to initiate authenticated remote control sessions on their host machine via slash commands. The plugin captures the resulting session URL and returns it to the user, enabling full access to local tools and databases from the Claude mobile app. Beyond session management, it supports headless command execution and direct shell access with built-in safety filters for dangerous operations.
highlights: Starts Claude Code remote control sessions via Telegram slash commands
Provides mobile access to local filesystems and MCP servers
Executes headless commands and shell operations on the host machine

AaronWong1999/hermesclaw.summary: HermesClaw is an integration bridge that enables a single WeChat account to run both Hermes Agent and OpenClaw simultaneously. It functions as a central iLink poller that creates two local proxy servers, allowing each agent gateway to receive messages without token conflicts or 403 errors. Users can toggle between agents or trigger simultaneous responses using commands like /hermes, /openclaw, or /both. The tool maintains native support for voice transcriptions and media files by forwarding raw iLink protocol messages to the respective gateways.
highlights: Enables dual-agent operation on a single WeChat iLink account
Routes messages via /hermes, /openclaw, and /both commands
Automated installation script patches existing gateway configurations

stephenschoettler/hermes-lcm.summary: Hermes-LCM is a Lossless Context Management plugin for Hermes Agent that prevents information loss during long-running conversations. It replaces standard lossy compression with a hierarchical Directed Acyclic Graph (DAG) that persists every message in an immutable-first SQLite store. When context limits are reached, the engine uses three levels of escalation—from detailed summaries to deterministic truncation—while providing the agent with specialized tools to retrieve original details. This architecture allows agents to maintain a bounded active context window without losing the ability to drill back into compacted history through structured retrieval.
highlights: Hierarchical DAG summaries with three-level escalation for context compaction
Immutable-first SQLite storage ensures no message data is ever deleted
Agent tools for grep, description, and expansion of compacted history

Codename-11/hermes-relay.summary: Hermes-Relay is a native Android companion application designed to provide a mobile interface for the Hermes agent platform. It establishes a connection between a mobile device and a Hermes server using a Python-based plugin and WebSockets (WSS) for real-time communication. Users can engage in chat, access a terminal, and manage device controls directly from their phone. The system simplifies setup through a QR-code pairing process that configures both the chat API and the relay server simultaneously.
highlights: Native Android client supporting chat and terminal interfaces
WebSocket-based relay for remote device control and monitoring
Streamlined QR-code pairing for automated server and plugin configuration

clawvader-tech/hermes-telegram-miniapp.summary: Hermes Telegram Mini App is a React-based terminal interface that allows users to manage and interact with Hermes agents directly within the Telegram ecosystem. It establishes a secure mobile-first workspace by wrapping the Hermes CLI in a dark-mode web UI, featuring streaming chat and system monitoring. The application utilizes dual HMAC-SHA256 and Ed25519 validation to ensure secure access via Telegram's authentication layer. Users can spawn background agent instances, manage scheduled cron jobs, and perform local OCR or vision analysis on uploaded files.
highlights: Terminal-style chat with streaming responses and slash command support
Background agent spawning with live output monitoring and interactive modes
Integrated system health gauges for CPU, memory, and disk usage

briancaffey/hermes-otel.summary: hermes-otel is an observability plugin for the Hermes Agent that enables comprehensive monitoring of agentic workflows. It automatically captures and exports LLM tool calls, model invocations, and API requests as OpenTelemetry spans to any OTLP-compatible backend. The plugin provides deep visibility into session lifecycles, including parent-child span nesting and token usage aggregation. It supports a wide range of observability platforms including Phoenix, Langfuse, SigNoz, and Grafana for tracking traces, metrics, and logs.
highlights: Exports LLM tool calls and model invocations as OTel spans
Supports multiple backends including Phoenix, Langfuse, and SigNoz
Aggregates token usage and session I/O metrics automatically

EKKOLearnAI/hermes-web-ui.summary: Hermes Web UI is a comprehensive management dashboard for the Hermes Agent ecosystem that provides a graphical interface for AI interaction and system administration. It centralizes agent operations by allowing users to manage multi-platform chat sessions, configure communication channels, and monitor resource usage through a unified web portal. The platform features integrated tools for scheduling cron jobs, browsing remote files, and managing model provider credentials across local and containerized environments. By automating gateway configuration and port resolution, it simplifies the deployment of autonomous agents for both personal and enterprise workflows.
highlights: Unified configuration for Telegram, Discord, Slack, WhatsApp, and Matrix channels
Real-time usage analytics with token tracking and cost estimation charts
Integrated web terminal and remote file browser for backend management

Web3CZ/Web3Hermes.summary: Web3Hermes is a lightweight WebUI for Hermes Agent specifically localized for Chinese-speaking users. It provides a desktop-optimized interface that simplifies interaction with the agent through a localized workflow and full Chinese language support. The platform integrates a built-in workspace file browser, session management tools, and support for multiple AI models including OpenAI, Anthropic, and DeepSeek. Users can manage tasks, edit code with syntax highlighting, and visualize tool calls within a responsive dark or light themed environment.
highlights: Localized Chinese interface with dark and light theme support
Integrated workspace file browser for direct file editing and management
Comprehensive session management including export, search, and tool call visualization

AxDSan/mnemosyne.summary: Mnemosyne is a local-first memory system designed to provide private, low-latency context for Hermes Agents. It utilizes a SQLite-backed architecture featuring native vector search and FTS5 full-text search to eliminate the need for external databases or API keys. The system implements a three-tier BEAM architecture that manages hot working memory, long-term episodic memory, and a temporary scratchpad. It includes automatic consolidation processes that summarize and move old memories to long-term storage during sleep cycles.
highlights: Sub-millisecond read and write latency using local SQLite storage
Hybrid search combining vector similarity, keyword ranking, and importance scores
BEAM architecture for tiered working, episodic, and scratchpad memory
